title: Single-Electrode, Bidirectional Control of Heart Rate via Vagus Nerve Modulation
author: Shane Bender

steps.title: Procure animals for experimentation
steps.instruction: Procure animals for experimentation

steps.title: This study used male Sprague-Dawley rats,...
steps.instruction: This study used male Sprague-Dawley rats, approximately 7–11 weeks old, weighing 400–600 g

steps.title: House according to institutional vivarium standards prior to experimentation
steps.instruction: House according to institutional vivarium standards prior to experimentation.

steps.title: Stimulating/blocking source
steps.instruction: Stimulating/blocking source: open-source COSMIIC project's GAMMA module

steps.title: Any pair of computer-controllable constant-current stimulators...
steps.instruction: Any pair of computer-controllable constant-current stimulators capable of 10kHz square waves can be used with modest changes to the software

steps.title: Stimulating/blocking electrodes
steps.instruction: Stimulating/blocking electrodes: custom-fabricated bipolar platinum electrodes, 1x1 mm windows

steps.title: Any electrode that fits on the...
steps.instruction: Any electrode that fits on the rat vagus nerve can be used

steps.title: ECG recording
steps.instruction: ECG recording: Amplifier: CED 1902 Isolated Amplifier with 1902-10 headstage Data Recording: CED Power 1401 and Spike2v10 software

steps.title: Recording system of your choice can be substituted
steps.instruction: Recording system of your choice can be substituted

steps.title: Controller Implementation
steps.instruction: Controller Implementation: NI USB-6363 BNC

steps.title: Any LabVIEW-compatable data acquisition device can...
steps.instruction: Any LabVIEW-compatable data acquisition device can be used with modest changes to the software

steps.title: Controller Software
steps.instruction: Controller Software: Windows PC with LabVIEW 2024

steps.title: Replacement software should follow a similar data flow
steps.instruction: Replacement software should follow a similar data flow: ECG acquisition → Heart Rate Calculation → Fuzzy Logic Controller → Output to current source

steps.title: Induce Rat Anesthesia
steps.instruction: Induce Rat Anesthesia

steps.title: Place rat in induction chamber
steps.instruction: Place rat in induction chamber

steps.title: Deliver isoflurane at 3-5% for 2-5...
steps.instruction: Deliver isoflurane at 3-5% for 2-5 minutes, until rat is unconcious

steps.title: Weigh rat and place in anesthesia...
steps.instruction: Weigh rat and place in anesthesia nose cone on heated pad

steps.title: Prepare animal skin while anesthesia gets deeper
steps.instruction: Prepare animal skin while anesthesia gets deeper

steps.title: While leaving the animal at 3-5%...
steps.instruction: While leaving the animal at 3-5% isoflurane, shave the ventral cervical neck, over the trachea

steps.title: Continuously monitor rat vital signs until...
steps.instruction: Continuously monitor rat vital signs until appropriate anesthesia depth for intubation is reached Minimum breathing rate is 20 breaths per minute Minimum heart rat is 200 beats per minute

steps.title: Intubate the animal
steps.instruction: Intubate the animal

steps.title: Ensure the animal is sufficiently sedated,...
steps.instruction: Ensure the animal is sufficiently sedated, such that they will not wake up without isoflurane for the duration of the intubation process, ~1-2 minutes

steps.title: Lay out your intubation board, large...
steps.instruction: Lay out your intubation board, large forceps, and laryngoscope, and 14-gauge angiocatheter (16-gauge may be used as well, but may result in air escaping through the larynx in larger animals

steps.title: Take the rat out of the...
steps.instruction: Take the rat out of the nose cone, and lay on your intubation board. Secure nose/upper jaw in place

steps.title: Use one hand to grab the...
steps.instruction: Use one hand to grab the tongue with the large forceps, and use your other hand to place the laryngoscope in the mouth, and visualize the larynx

steps.title: Once you can see the opening...
steps.instruction: Once you can see the opening to the trachea, put down the forceps and use that hand to insert the angiocatheter into the trachea

steps.title: Connect the angiocatheter to a ventillator,...
steps.instruction: Connect the angiocatheter to a ventillator, hook the ventilator up to the anesthesia, and begin ventillation at 4-5 mL stroke volume, 60 breaths per minute.

steps.title: Ventilator
steps.instruction: Ventilator: Harvard Apparatus Model 683

steps.title: Any equivalent rodent vetilator will suffice...
steps.instruction: Any equivalent rodent vetilator will suffice (max 5 mL stroke volume, 60 breaths per minute)

steps.title: Transfer rat back to heated pad...
steps.instruction: Transfer rat back to heated pad in a supine position, being careful not to pull out the angiocatheter

steps.title: Expose the vagus on each side
steps.instruction: Expose the vagus on each side

steps.title: Make two rostral-caudal incisions in the...
steps.instruction: Make two rostral-caudal incisions in the skin, one on each side of the trachea over the vagus nerves

steps.title: Connect the incisions across to form an "H" shape
steps.instruction: Connect the incisions across to form an "H" shape

steps.title: Blunt dissect to isolate the vagus...
steps.instruction: Blunt dissect to isolate the vagus nerves, often bundled with the carotid artery and jugular vein

steps.title: Cuff the Vagus
steps.instruction: Cuff the Vagus

steps.title: Place two cuff electrodes on the...
steps.instruction: Place two cuff electrodes on the right vagus nerve, leaving space between them and ensuring that they do not pull on or kink the nerve

steps.title: Isolate the system and remove tonic activity
steps.instruction: Isolate the system and remove tonic activity

steps.title: Use fine forceps to crush the...
steps.instruction: Use fine forceps to crush the right side nerve proximal to the electrodes. This will keep the nerve structurally intact to prevent the nerve from getting pulled out of the electrodes

steps.title: The left vagus can be similarly crushed, or cut completely
steps.instruction: The left vagus can be similarly crushed, or cut completely

steps.title: Calibrate stimulation slope for the control (distal) electrode
steps.instruction: Calibrate stimulation slope for the control (distal) electrode

steps.title: Find an amplitude for your stimulation...
steps.instruction: Find an amplitude for your stimulation that elicits a maximal heart rate decrease (typically tested at 30 Hz, 50 μs per phase, currents 0.1 mA to 5 mA peak-to-peak expected) If AV block is seen (heart rate drops roughly in half suddenly), back off of the amplitude until normal conduction resumes

steps.title: Record the baseline heart rate with no stimulation
steps.instruction: Record the baseline heart rate with no stimulation

steps.title: Increase the control electrode stimulation in...
steps.instruction: Increase the control electrode stimulation in steps of 1 Hz, and plot the change in heart rate

steps.title: Once the heart rate stops changing,...
steps.instruction: Once the heart rate stops changing, fit a linear regression to the data and obtain the slope in bpm/Hz

steps.title: Find the stimulation amplitude for the perturbation (proximal) electrode
steps.instruction: Find the stimulation amplitude for the perturbation (proximal) electrode

steps.title: Using the same procedure as above,...
steps.instruction: Using the same procedure as above, find the maximal stimulation amplitude at 30 Hz, 50 μs per phase for the proximal electrode

steps.title: Calibrate the block start and slope...
steps.instruction: Calibrate the block start and slope for the control (distal) electrode

steps.title: Apply maximal stimulation to elicit bradycardia on the perturbation electrode
steps.instruction: Apply maximal stimulation to elicit bradycardia on the perturbation electrode

steps.title: Increase the amplitude of a 10...
steps.instruction: Increase the amplitude of a 10 kHz square wave blocking waveform on the control electrode until you see the heart rate start to increase towards baseline. This is you block starting current.

steps.title: Continue increasing the amplitude of the...
steps.instruction: Continue increasing the amplitude of the block, and plotting the resultant heart rate

steps.title: Once the heart rate reaches the...
steps.instruction: Once the heart rate reaches the baseline heart rate (pre-perturbation-stimulation), fit a line to your data and record the blocking slope in bpm/mA

steps.title: Enter these calibration values into the controller
steps.instruction: Enter these calibration values into the controller

steps.title: Begin recording data for each trial
steps.instruction: Begin recording data for each trial

steps.title: Before using the controller, briefly apply...
steps.instruction: Before using the controller, briefly apply 30 Hz stimulation to the control electrode (~30 s)

steps.title: Compare the stimulation bradycardia heart rate...
steps.instruction: Compare the stimulation bradycardia heart rate (0%) and the post-stimulation baseline heart rate (100%)

steps.title: Your controller setpoint will be halfway between these values (50%)
steps.instruction: Your controller setpoint will be halfway between these values (50%)

steps.title: Allow for at least 2 minutes...
steps.instruction: Allow for at least 2 minutes of baseline recording before turning controller on

steps.title: Turn the controller on and allow...
steps.instruction: Turn the controller on and allow the controller to apply stimulation to the control electrode to bring the heart rate down to the 50% setpoint for 2 minutes

steps.title: Set the desired controller Gain (2x, 5x, or 10x)
steps.instruction: Set the desired controller Gain (2x, 5x, or 10x)

steps.title: After two minutes of the controller...
steps.instruction: After two minutes of the controller being on, start ramping the stimulation frequency on the perturbation electrode

steps.title: Ramp up to 30 Hz in...
steps.instruction: Ramp up to 30 Hz in 5 minutes, then ramp back down to 1 Hz in another 5 mintues

steps.title: After the perturbation ramp is done,...
steps.instruction: After the perturbation ramp is done, allow the controller to run for an additional 2 minutes

steps.title: At the end of the trial,...
steps.instruction: At the end of the trial, turn off the controller, and let the heart rate return to baseline

steps.title: Repeat this protocol for the other two gain values
steps.instruction: Repeat this protocol for the other two gain values

steps.title: To euthanize the animal, turn your...
steps.instruction: To euthanize the animal, turn your anesthesia up to 5%, and inject sodium pentobarbital into the heart with a 22-gauge needle

steps.title: This should be substituted with whatever...
steps.instruction: This should be substituted with whatever euthanasia method your institution has approved for terminal experiments
